data.description: OOREP is an open online repertory for homeopathy. You can find out more info on https://www.oorep.com/. - nondeterministic/oorep

Afterwards change into\nthe \u003ccode\u003edocker\u003c/code\u003e subdirectory of your then local source code repository and execute the\nfollowing two commands one after another:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"$ docker-compose pull\n$ docker-compose up\"\u003e\u003cpre class=\"notranslate\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003e$ docker-compose pull\n$ docker-compose up\n\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eDepending on your Internet connection, those commands may take a while to complete.\nWhen all is done, you should be able to point your web browser to \u003ccode\u003ehttp://localhost:9000\u003c/code\u003e\nand see OOREP's landing page. Needless to say, those images are test-only and are not\nintended for any production environments or the like without further modifications.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eJava SDK \u0026gt;= v17\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eScala Build Tool SBT \u0026gt;= v1.9.7\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eA PostgreSQL database server \u0026gt;= v11\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eBefore executing SBT, the database (which is provided here as an SQL dump) needs\nto be up and running and PostgreSQL's pgcrypto-extension created (e.g., as\nsuperuser \u003ccode\u003epostgres\u003c/code\u003e, issue the command \u003ccode\u003eCREATE EXTENSION pgcrypto;\u003c/code\u003e on the\nOOREP-database; if you still encounter permission issues when restoring the database,\nyou may also want to try \u003ccode\u003eALTER ROLE \"\u0026lt;your username\u0026gt;\" superuser;\u003c/code\u003e). Also, you will\nneed to define the following environment variables:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003e$OOREP_APP_PROTOCOL\u003c/code\u003e: usually either \u003ccode\u003ehttp\u003c/code\u003e or \u003ccode\u003ehttps\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003e$OOREP_APP_HOSTNAME\u003c/code\u003e: the part that follows \u003ccode\u003e$OOREP_APP_PROTOCOL\u003c/code\u003e,\nsuch as \u003ccode\u003ewww\u003c/code\u003e, for example, or any other hostname\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003e$OOREP_APP_DOMAIN\u003c/code\u003e: your domain name, e.g., \u003ccode\u003eoorep.com\u003c/code\u003e, but do not set when, for\nexample, you're merely using \u003ccode\u003elocalhost\u003c/code\u003e as hostname\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003e$OOREP_APP_PORT\u003c/code\u003e: port of application server (normally \u003ccode\u003e9000\u003c/code\u003e), but should only be\nset if application server is directly used (i.e., do not set when using a reverse\nproxy!)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003e$OOREP_APP_SECRET\u003c/code\u003e: application-specific password you need to set if you run the\napplication server using \u003ccode\u003ehttps\u003c/code\u003e instead of \u003ccode\u003ehttp\u003c/code\u003e (which I don't, since I use a\nreverse proxy that does end-to-end encryption)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003e$OOREP_DB_NAME\u003c/code\u003e: name of your PostgreSQL database; set to \u003ccode\u003eoorep\u003c/code\u003e, unless\nyou know better!\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003e$OOREP_DB_USER\u003c/code\u003e: name of your PostgreSQL database user; set to \u003ccode\u003eoorep_user\u003c/code\u003e, unless\nyou know better!\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003e$OOREP_DB_PASS\u003c/code\u003e: password of your PostgreSQL database; for test-environments,\nuse the one from within the \u003ccode\u003edocker\u003c/code\u003e directory of this repository, unless you know better!\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003e$OOREP_DB_HOST\u003c/code\u003e: full hostname of DB-server (e.g., \u003ccode\u003elocalhost\u003c/code\u003e or \u003ccode\u003edb.oorep.com\u003c/code\u003e)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003e$OOREP_DB_PORT\u003c/code\u003e: port of DB-server (usually \u003ccode\u003e5432\u003c/code\u003e)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003e$OOREP_URL_LOGOUT\u003c/code\u003e: right now only mandatory if authentication is used:\nthe URL which users must click on in order to logout of OOREP (usually your\nlogout binding of an accompanying (SAML) Service Provider)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003e$OOREP_MAIL_SERVER\u003c/code\u003e: right now only mandatory if authentication is used:\nhostname of your SMTP server to send password reset mails (e.g., mail.server.com)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003e$OOREP_MAIL_USER\u003c/code\u003e: right now only mandatory if authentication is used:\nusername on your SMTP server\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003e$OOREP_MAIL_PASS\u003c/code\u003e: right now only mandatory if authentication is used:\npassword for that username on your SMTP server\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eCheck \u003ccode\u003ebackend/conf/application.conf\u003c/code\u003e for their respective use, and make other\nadjustments as you see fit there (especially if you alter the domain variable).\nAlso be sure these variables are set before attempting to execute the OOREP code\nusing SBT.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv font\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eFor your convenience, an archived package with the respective versions\nis available \u003ca href=\"http://pspace.org/a/third-party-v3.tar.gz\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003ehere\u003c/a\u003e.  It should\nbe unpacked and then placed inside \u003ccode\u003ebackend/public/html\u003c/code\u003e within the main\nOOREP directory. (For OOREP versions \u0026lt; 0.11, you should use\n\u003ca href=\"http://pspace.org/a/third-party.tar.gz\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003ethis file\u003c/a\u003e\ninstead.)\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eCompiling and running\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-compiling-and-running\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Compiling and running\" href=\"#compiling-and-running\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eWhen you're content with your setup, OOREP, a\n\u003ca href=\"https://www.playframework.com/\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003ePlay\u003c/a\u003e application written in Scala, can be run\nlike any other using SBT's targets \u003ccode\u003erun\u003c/code\u003e or \u003ccode\u003ecompile\u003c/code\u003e.  Be sure, the database is\nstarted and available before though. If all went well, the result should then be\navailable at \u003ca href=\"http://localhost:9000/\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003ehttp://localhost:9000/\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eYou can also build a distribution package of OOREP by first executing \u003ccode\u003ecompile\u003c/code\u003e\nand then \u003ccode\u003edist\u003c/code\u003e in SBT, which will build an executable,\n\u003ccode\u003ebackend/target/universal/backend-x.y.z.zip\u003c/code\u003e, and which can also be used to run\na stand-alone version of the application. This is also how the oorep.com-server\nis run.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eRuntime optimisations\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-runtime-optimisations\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Runtime optimisations\" href=\"#runtime-optimisations\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eSome queries will be slow, if the database is not optimised. As this is\nwork in progress, the following is a somewhat crude approximation: It is\nadvisable to add the following (and possibly other) indexes to OOREP's\ndatabase:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"create index on rubricremedy (abbrev, remedyid);\ncreate index on rubricremedy (abbrev, rubricid);\ncreate index on rubricremedy (abbrev, rubricid, remedyid);\ncreate index on remedy (nameabbrev, id);\ncreate index on rubric (abbrev, id);\"\u003e\u003cpre users. Instead, the OOREP application expects the variable \u003ccode\u003eX-Remote-User\u003c/code\u003e to be set\nin the HTTP request header then containing the OOREP user-ID.  There are various ways to\nachieve  this.  In \u003ca href=\"http://www.oorep.com\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003ewww.oorep.com\u003c/a\u003e SAML is used as follows: all calls to OOREP are routed\nthrough a SAML service provider which protects calls to \u003ccode\u003e/login\u003c/code\u003e and \u003ccode\u003e/api/sec/...\u003c/code\u003e by\ninvoking  a SAML identity provider. When the user enters valid  credentials to the\nidentity provider, this variable is set and passed back to OOREP's service provider.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eFor this to work securely, you need to make sure that, indeed, all calls to \u003ccode\u003e/login\u003c/code\u003e and\n\u003ccode\u003e/api/sec/...\u003c/code\u003e are safe-guarded by the service provider, e.g., by adding a\n\u003ccode\u003e\u0026lt;LocationMatch /(login|api/sec/.+)\u0026gt;\u003c/code\u003e directive to an Apache2 reverse-proxy (or similar -\ndepending on your setup), which passes such calls on to the service provider before they\nreach the main OOREP application server.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eWhile to this end \u003ca href=\"http://www.oorep.com\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003ewww.oorep.com\u003c/a\u003e uses a SAML solution, other authentication protocol\nimplementations can also be used. Also, it is probably not easy to get all this to work\nwithout using the aforementioned reverse-proxy, although it is not strictly an container-lg" itemprop="text"><p dir="auto"><a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" href="/nondeterministic/oorep/blob/master/doc/logo.png"><img src="/nondeterministic/oorep/raw/master/doc/logo.png" alt="Logo" title="OOREP" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></p>
<p dir="auto">OOREP is an acronym for open online repertory for homeopathy.  That is, it lets
users look up categories in homeopathic repertories.  This git repository
consists of its (mainly) Scala source code.  A running version of it, however,
can also be found at <a href="https://www.oorep.com/" rel="nofollow">https://www.oorep.com/</a>.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Current status / what's new</h2><a id="user-content-current-status--whats-new" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Current status / what's new" href="#current-status--whats-new"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Besides the aforementioned homepage, check out the <a href="/nondeterministic/oorep/blob/master/NEWS">NEWS</a> file for the
latest development updates. (You may want to to focus on releases marked as 'stable'.)</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Running the program</h2><a id="user-content-running-the-program" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Running the program" href="#running-the-program"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">You can choose between the following two different methods in order to get OOREP
up and running on your own machine.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Method 1: By using ready-made Docker images (recommended method)</h3><a id="user-content-method-1-by-using-ready-made-docker-images-recommended-method" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Method 1: By using ready-made Docker images (recommended method)" href="#method-1-by-using-ready-made-docker-images-recommended-method"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Prerequisite</h4><a id="user-content-prerequisite" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Prerequisite" href="#prerequisite"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>You have the commands <code>docker</code> and <code>docker-compose</code> available on your machine.</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Pull and start the Docker images from <a href="https://hub.docker.com/u/oorep" rel="nofollow">Dockerhub</a></h4><a id="user-content-pull-and-start-the-docker-images-from-dockerhub" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Pull and start the Docker images from Dockerhub" href="#pull-and-start-the-docker-images-from-dockerhub"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">But first, clone OOREP's source code repository from here. Afterwards change into
the <code>docker</code> subdirectory of your then local source code repository and execute the
following two commands one after another:</p>
<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content="$ docker-compose pull
$ docker-compose up"><pre class="notranslate"><code>$ docker-compose pull
$ docker-compose up
</code></pre></div>
<p dir="auto">Depending on your Internet connection, those commands may take a while to complete.
When all is done, you should be able to point your web browser to <code>http://localhost:9000</code>
and see OOREP's landing page. Needless to say, those images are test-only and are not
intended for any production environments or the like without further modifications.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Method 2: By building the project from scratch (for experts only)</h3><a id="user-content-method-2-by-building-the-project-from-scratch-for-experts-only" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Method 2: By building the project from scratch (for experts only)" href="#method-2-by-building-the-project-from-scratch-for-experts-only"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Prerequisites</h4><a id="user-content-prerequisites" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Prerequisites" href="#prerequisites"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Java SDK >= v17</li>
<li>Scala Build Tool SBT >= v1.9.7</li>
<li>A PostgreSQL database server >= v11</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Before executing SBT, the database (which is provided here as an SQL dump) needs
to be up and running and PostgreSQL's pgcrypto-extension created (e.g., as
superuser <code>postgres</code>, issue the command <code>CREATE EXTENSION pgcrypto;</code> on the
OOREP-database; if you still encounter permission issues when restoring the database,
you may also want to try <code>ALTER ROLE "<your username>" superuser;</code>). Also, you will
need to define the following environment variables:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>$OOREP_APP_PROTOCOL</code>: usually either <code>http</code> or <code>https</code></li>
<li><code>$OOREP_APP_HOSTNAME</code>: the part that follows <code>$OOREP_APP_PROTOCOL</code>,
such as <code>www</code>, for example, or any other hostname</li>
<li><code>$OOREP_APP_DOMAIN</code>: your domain name, e.g., <code>oorep.com</code>, but do not set when, for
example, you're merely using <code>localhost</code> as hostname</li>
<li><code>$OOREP_APP_PORT</code>: port of application server (normally <code>9000</code>), but should only be
set if application server is directly used (i.e., do not set when using a reverse
proxy!)</li>
<li><code>$OOREP_APP_SECRET</code>: application-specific password you need to set if you run the
application server using <code>https</code> instead of <code>http</code> (which I don't, since I use a
reverse proxy that does end-to-end encryption)</li>
<li><code>$OOREP_DB_NAME</code>: name of your PostgreSQL database; set to <code>oorep</code>, unless
you know better!</li>
<li><code>$OOREP_DB_USER</code>: name of your PostgreSQL database user; set to <code>oorep_user</code>, unless
you know better!</li>
<li><code>$OOREP_DB_PASS</code>: password of your PostgreSQL database; for test-environments,
use the one from within the <code>docker</code> directory of this repository, unless you know better!</li>
<li><code>$OOREP_DB_HOST</code>: full hostname of DB-server (e.g., <code>localhost</code> or <code>db.oorep.com</code>)</li>
<li><code>$OOREP_DB_PORT</code>: port of DB-server (usually <code>5432</code>)</li>
<li><code>$OOREP_URL_LOGOUT</code>: right now only mandatory if authentication is used:
the URL which users must click on in order to logout of OOREP (usually your
logout binding of an accompanying (SAML) Service Provider)</li>
<li><code>$OOREP_MAIL_SERVER</code>: right now only mandatory if authentication is used:
hostname of your SMTP server to send password reset mails (e.g., mail.server.com)</li>
<li><code>$OOREP_MAIL_USER</code>: right now only mandatory if authentication is used:
username on your SMTP server</li>
<li><code>$OOREP_MAIL_PASS</code>: right now only mandatory if authentication is used:
password for that username on your SMTP server</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Check <code>backend/conf/application.conf</code> for their respective use, and make other
adjustments as you see fit there (especially if you alter the domain variable).
Also be sure these variables are set before attempting to execute the OOREP code
using SBT.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Recommended external libraries</h4><a id="user-content-recommended-external-libraries" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Recommended external libraries" href="#recommended-external-libraries"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">It is strongly recommended to also download the following external JavaScript
libraries and fonts:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><a href="https://github.com/michalsnik/aos">aos</a> (tested with v2.3.4)</li>
<li><a href="https://getbootstrap.com/" rel="nofollow">bootstrap</a> (tested with v4.3.1)</li>
<li><a href="https://bootswatch.com/" rel="nofollow">bootswatch</a> (tested with v4.3.1)</li>
<li><a href="https://jquery.com/" rel="nofollow">jquery</a> (tested with v3.5.1)</li>
<li><a href="https://useiconic.com/open" rel="nofollow">Open Iconic font</a></li>
<li><a href="https://fonts.google.com/specimen/Open+Sans" rel="nofollow">Open Sans font</a></li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Linux_Libertine" rel="nofollow">Linux Libertine Display font</a></li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">For your convenience, an archived package with the respective versions
is available <a href="http://pspace.org/a/third-party-v3.tar.gz" rel="nofollow">here</a>.  It should
be unpacked and then placed inside <code>backend/public/html</code> within the main
OOREP directory. (For OOREP versions < 0.11, you should use
<a href="http://pspace.org/a/third-party.tar.gz" rel="nofollow">this file</a>
instead.)</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Compiling and running</h4><a id="user-content-compiling-and-running" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Compiling and running" href="#compiling-and-running"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">When you're content with your setup, OOREP, a
<a href="https://www.playframework.com/" rel="nofollow">Play</a> application written in Scala, can be run
like any other using SBT's targets <code>run</code> or <code>compile</code>.  Be sure, the database is
started and available before though. If all went well, the result should then be
available at <a href="http://localhost:9000/" rel="nofollow">http://localhost:9000/</a>.</p>
<p dir="auto">You can also build a distribution package of OOREP by first executing <code>compile</code>
and then <code>dist</code> in SBT, which will build an executable,
<code>backend/target/universal/backend-x.y.z.zip</code>, and which can also be used to run
a stand-alone version of the application. This is also how the oorep.com-server
is run.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Runtime optimisations</h4><a id="user-content-runtime-optimisations" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Runtime optimisations" href="#runtime-optimisations"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Some queries will be slow, if the database is not optimised. As this is
work in progress, the following is a somewhat crude approximation: It is
advisable to add the following (and possibly other) indexes to OOREP's
database:</p>
<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content="create index on rubricremedy (abbrev, remedyid);
create index on rubricremedy (abbrev, rubricid);
create index on rubricremedy (abbrev, rubricid, remedyid);
create index on remedy (nameabbrev, id);
create index on rubric (abbrev, id);"><pre class="notranslate"><code>create index on rubricremedy (abbrev, remedyid);
create index on rubricremedy (abbrev, rubricid);
create index on rubricremedy (abbrev, rubricid, remedyid);
create index on remedy (nameabbrev, id);
create index on rubric (abbrev, id);
</code></pre></div>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Making user login work</h2><a id="user-content-making-user-login-work" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Making user login work" href="#making-user-login-work"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Since version v0.10.0, OOREP no longer provides its own code to handle login and logout
of users. Instead, the OOREP application expects the variable <code>X-Remote-User</code> to be set
in the HTTP request header then containing the OOREP user-ID.  There are various ways to
achieve  this.  In <a href="http://www.oorep.com" rel="nofollow">www.oorep.com</a> SAML is used as follows: all calls to OOREP are routed
through a SAML service provider which protects calls to <code>/login</code> and <code>/api/sec/...</code> by
invoking  a SAML identity provider. When the user enters valid  credentials to the
identity provider, this variable is set and passed back to OOREP's service provider.</p>
<p dir="auto">For this to work securely, you need to make sure that, indeed, all calls to <code>/login</code> and
<code>/api/sec/...</code> are safe-guarded by the service provider, e.g., by adding a
<code><LocationMatch /(login|api/sec/.+)></code> directive to an Apache2 reverse-proxy (or similar -
depending on your setup), which passes such calls on to the service provider before they
reach the main OOREP application server.</p>
<p dir="auto">While to this end <a href="http://www.oorep.com" rel="nofollow">www.oorep.com</a> uses a SAML solution, other authentication protocol
implementations can also be used. Also, it is probably not easy to get all this to work
without using the aforementioned reverse-proxy, although it is not strictly an OOREP
requirement.</p>
</article></div></div></div></div></div></div></div><div class="prc-PageLayout-PaneWrapper-pHPop pr-2" style="--offset-header:0px;--spacing-row:var(--spacing-none);--spacing-column:var(--spacing-none)" data-is-hidden="false" data-position="end"><div class="prc-PageLayout-HorizontalDivider-JLVqp prc-PageLayout-PaneHorizontalDivider-9tbnE" data-component="PageLayout.HorizontalDivider" data-variant-narrow="none" data-variant-regular="none" data-position="end" style="--spacing-divider:var(--spacing-none);--spacing:var(--spacing-none)"></div><div class="prc-PageLayout-Pane-AyzHK" data-component="PageLayout.Pane" style="--spacing:var(--spacing-normal);--pane-min-width:256px;--pane-max-width:calc(100vw - var(--pane-max-width-diff));--pane-width-size:var(--pane-width-large);--pane-width:320px"><rails-partial data-partial-name="codeViewRepoRoute.Sidebar" class="RailsPartial-module__d-contents__G5m4w">
